---
type: model_ledger
language: en
generated: <YYYY-MM-DD>
model_id: <model id, copied verbatim from what your runtime states this session — your Kimi session reports it where available; "unrecorded" only if the session names none>
model_trail:                    # append-only: one entry per write session, never rewritten
  - { date: <YYYY-MM-DD>, model: <model id or "unrecorded">, skill: <star-…>, scope: <what this session wrote> }
artifacts_scanned: <n>
artifacts_without_trail: <n>
---

# Model Ledger

<!-- Written by /skill:star-expt-digest ledger. This file is GENERATED by rolling up the model_trail of
     every registered artifact on disk — it is never hand-maintained, and editing it here changes
     nothing. To correct a row, fix the trail in the artifact it came from and regenerate.
     Every value is copied from a trail; nothing here is inferred. Trails are self-reported
     (conventions §8), so this ledger inherits that limit: it reports what each runtime claimed,
     not verified provenance. -->

## 1. Summary

<!-- Three or four sentences: how many artifacts were scanned, how many carried a trail, how many
     distinct models appear, the date range covered, and how many artifacts are single-model versus
     multi-model. State plainly that a model with few write events is not evidence of anything —
     this is a provenance record, not a benchmark. -->

## 2. Write Events

<!-- One row per model_trail entry across every artifact, newest first. This is the flow: the same
     artifact appears once per session that wrote it, so a file handed between models shows as
     consecutive rows with different models. Write "None" if no artifact carries a trail yet. -->

| Date | Model | Skill | Artifact | Scope |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <YYYY-MM-DD> | `<model id>` | `star-plan-executor` | `wkdrs/<run>/EXEC_LOG.md` | steps 4–5, finalize |

## 3. Multi-Model Artifacts

<!-- Only artifacts whose trail names more than one distinct model — the handoffs. These are the
     rows worth reading when comparing models, because the same file carries both models' work.
     Show the models in the order they wrote. Write "None" if every artifact had a single author. -->

| Artifact | Models in order | Sessions | First → Last |
|---|---|---|---|
| `metds/plans/<prefix>_<slug>_plan.md` | `<model a>` → `<model b>` | 3 | <YYYY-MM-DD> → <YYYY-MM-DD> |

## 4. Per-Model Totals

<!-- Write events per model, by artifact kind. Counts only — never a quality judgment, and never a
     rate ("X wrote more" is not "X did better"). The kinds are the conventions §8 registry rows. -->

| Model | Plans | Runs | Reports | Compiled docs | Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `<model id>` | 2 | 5 | 3 | 1 | 11 |

## 5. Gaps

<!-- Registered artifacts on disk carrying no model_trail (written before the field existed, or by a
     skill that skipped it), and trail entries whose model is `unrecorded`. These are the blind spots
     in the ledger; a reader must be able to see how much is missing before drawing a conclusion.
     Write "None" if every artifact is fully attributed. -->

| Artifact | Gap | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| `metds/codearc.md` | no `model_trail` | predates the field — its authorship is unknown, not single-model |
